events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: windshield replaced with forward-facing camera bracket. OEM requires static target calibration after glass replacement. Installer road-tested, no dash warnings, released vehicle. Shop lacks calibration targets, customer declined dealer visit.

This is non-compliant. OEM procedure specifies static target calibration. Absence of warning lights doesn't mean calibration is correct. Releasing vehicle without required calibration is non-compliant and unsafe. Decision: fail. Risk: high (ADAS system may not function correctly, safety risk). Component conditions: non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 80 (glass installed but calibration not done). Value: 0. Confidence high. Findings: ADAS calibration not performed per OEM requirement. Actions: perform static target calibration before release, or refer to facility with proper targets. Rationale: no warning lights doesn't verify calibration; OEM requires static calibration after glass replacement. References: OEM position statements, OEM service procedure.
